title: After-Action Report — Weather Model & Newspaper-Style Box Score Export
scope: Two intertwined changes: 1. Weather model — game-conditions context drawn at schedule time and surfaced everywhere a game is rendered. 2. Newspaper-style box score — once the engine surface was right, the markdown box-score export needed a render rewrite.

rationale.item: User specified: five categorical variables with 3–4 tiers each; drawn at SCHEDULE time, stamped on the games row; city × month climatological lookup via 7 procedural archetypes; engine touch points limited to prob.py, fielding, state.py — bounded, no sprinkling; magnitude budget [0.85, 1.20] with extreme weather ≤ ~10% movement; box-score weather strip.

The user's actual complaint was "the markdown file situation was copying an older version of the box score template" — markdown pipe-tables render as HTML grids in every viewer that styles tables, which is the database-dump look they were trying to escape.

delegation_events.decision: Weather model spec (variables, draw timing, archetypes, bounded engine seams, magnitude budget, scope exclusions)
delegation_events.quote: User specified: Five categorical variables (temperature, wind, humidity, precipitation, cloud cover) with 3–4 tiers each.

delegation_events.decision: Template-only W/L marker, no schema change
delegation_events.quote: The user said template-only — no new `is_winner` column.

delegation_events.decision: Pivot to fixing the markdown export rather than the HTML page
delegation_events.quote: The user pointed out the live HTML page is fine — the *markdown export* ("Copy as Markdown") is what was rendering as a database-dump grid because it still used `_md_table()` everywhere.

actions.description: New weather module: Weather dataclass, 5 tier vocabularies, archetype_for_city(), draw_weather(), 5 multiplier helpers; 7 hand-keyed archetypes from the team catalogue
Schedule-time weather draw: seed_schedule SELECTs city, forks RNG (rng_seed ^ 0xBA5EBA11), stamps 5 tier strings via extended INSERT
Schema: five new TEXT columns on games (temperature_tier, wind_tier, humidity_tier, precip_tier, cloud_tier) with ALTER-TABLE migrations, neutral-tier defaults

Bounded engine wiring (five reads): _pitch_probs (stamina decay + K multiplier), contact_quality (hard-share), resolve_contact (HR multiplier on park_hr + error roll), GameState.weather field, sim stamps state.weather
Calibration test simming 20 games each under HR-friendly extreme, HR-killer extreme, and neutral, with ratio envelopes
HTML weather strip under the scoreboard via Weather.from_row(game).short_label()

Newspaper-style monospace box-score renderer (66-col rule, dot leaders, F. Lastname names, annotation sections, W/L heuristic, weather + seed footer); export_box_score delegates to it wrapped in a code fence

risk_posture: Engine touch points limited to prob.py (HR / contact / K), fielding (errors), state.py (pitcher stamina decay) — bounded, no sprinkling.
Magnitude budget: individual multipliers in [0.85, 1.20]. Calibration test: extreme weather every game ≤ ~10% movement on league averages.
All multipliers collapse to 1.0 at NEUTRAL.
defaults are the neutral tiers so legacy rows are score-identical.
Weather lives on GameState.weather (Optional[object]) so legacy callers that don't supply one stay neutral.
No reads outside the five seams above.
Started looser (HR_WIND out=1.12, HR_TEMP hot=1.08) but the calibration test forced a tighter retune so an HR-friendly extreme stack lands inside ~+11% rather than ~+26%.

validation_claims.claim: Calibration test (tests/test_weather_calibration.py): passing. Confirms 20-game extreme-stack samples stay inside the envelope.

Smoke test: 30-team league seeded, 2,430 games scheduled, every game row has weather columns populated, first 3 games sim cleanly, HTML page renders with the weather strip, markdown export round-trips to monospace plaintext with the weather footer
validation_claims.check_method: Seed a 30-team league and inspect games rows, the detail page, and the export

validation_claims.claim: Pre-existing failures in o27v2/tests/test_phase8_db_migration.py and tests/test_stat_invariants.py (no DB) are unrelated — confirmed by stashing changes and re-running
validation_claims.check_method: Stash and re-run the failing tests

non_actions.reason: draw_weather() is purely simulated; adding one is a separate task and would need to handle stale data and rate limits
Wind is out/in/cross/neutral relative to the batter, not the field. Each park orientation entry would have to be authored.
Heavy rain is a flag — the game still plays. The error-rate multiplier captures the "wet ball" effect.
Every park is treated as outdoor at v1.
No attendance model exists, so weather does not feed one.

non_actions.item: Per-batter score progression (proper W/L, hold, save, blown-save tracking)
non_actions.reason: The current heuristic gets the box score's W/L marker on the page without it.
those vary too much day-to-day for a small table to capture meaningfully and the engine effects are small

residual_risks: W/L heuristic is wrong on close games where the W properly belongs to the bridge arm rather than the closer; stable, deterministic, reads sensibly.
Wind direction is field-agnostic — a "wind out" day in San Francisco and at Wrigley have the same HR multiplier.
heavy rain still plays — whether it should blow up into a real schedule disruption is a design question for v2.
Surname >9 chars still truncates (F. Verylongname overflows the 13-char field). Rare in practice.
open_hooks: Real W/L logic (pitcher-of-record at the final lead change) would need per-batter score tracking.
Park-orientation modeling is the next layer.
When indoor stadiums become a concept, pin those games to mild/neutral/normal/none/clear.
Widening the name field is cheap (rule + every subsequent column shifts by N).
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Weather Model & Newspaper-Style Box Score Export
unmapped_content: Tier vocabulary table (temperature cold/mild/warm/hot; wind out/neutral/in/cross; humidity dry/normal/humid; precipitation none/light/heavy; cloud clear/overcast/dusk). Archetypes: desert, coastal_cool, coastal_warm, continental_cold, continental_warm, tropical, mountain; unknown city falls back to continental_warm; months bucketed Apr–Sep (~7×6 tables). Final extreme-stack multipliers table (HR ×1.114/×0.876; hard contact ×1.020/×0.941; K ×1.040/×0.980; errors ×1.256; stamina decay ×1.113) with note that errors are rare so 25% moves league error rate a fraction of a point. Calibration envelopes: HR/PA (0.70, 1.30), H/AB (0.92, 1.10), K/PA (0.88, 1.12). box_text.py layout sample and column widths (name field 13 chars, rule width 68). Name format F. Lastname per AP-wire convention, replacing a full-name truncate approach (Kevin Aviles → Kevin Avile). W/L heuristic rules. Weather footer: representative °F per tier (cold 52 / mild 66 / warm 78 / hot 90); humidity descriptor suppressed when precipitation reported. Note on user's 'stamina decay in state.py' intent: ramp computation lives in prob.py:_pitch_probs, multiplier applied at that seam. Engine-seams table. Commits: weather model a5d4056; box-score rewrite this PR; branch claude/add-weather-model-qJ2sl.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed: 2026-05-05'. Three-stage AAR with an explicit 'What was deliberately left out' section.
